title: fix: check response content-type before SSE parsing for billing
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Filtering the data stream
gist.blurb: We added a new net to catch confused upstream responses before they get cached. Your text streams should flow much smoother now.
gist.summary: Added strict content-type validation for streaming requests to prevent malformed upstream responses from being cached.
gist.impact: Developers relying on text streaming will see improved reliability and no longer receive broken, cached JSON blobs when expecting a stream.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing a tiny fisher hat, using a net to catch bad data packets from a literal glowing green stream of water/data. The Nomnom creature sits on the bank eating the bad packets.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny fisher hat, using a net to catch broken code blocks from a flowing, glowing lime green (#ecf874) digital stream. The round Nomnom creature sits on the riverbank, happily eating the scooped-up bad data. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
